does: This paper introduces CHAI, a physical adversarial attack method that performs command hijacking against embodied AI systems by embedding deceptive natural language instructions in visual inputs. The attack exploits multimodal language interpretation abilities of large vision-language models through indirect prompt injection, systematically searching token space to build dictionaries of visual attack prompts that guide attacker models. The authors evaluate CHAI on four embodied AI agents including drone emergency landing, autonomous driving, aerial object tracking, and a real robotic vehicle, demonstrating that their approach consistently outperforms existing adversarial attacks.
connects: This work builds on growing research in adversarial attacks against vision-language models and robotic systems, extending from earlier studies on prompt injection attacks and adversarial examples in computer vision. It responds to the increasing deployment of large language models and multimodal foundation models in embodied AI and robotics, where grounding language understanding in visual perception has become standard. The paper connects to prior work on safety vulnerabilities in AI systems that leverage common-sense reasoning and generalization capabilities, while introducing the specific threat vector of manipulating physical environments to create adversarial visual inputs that deceive multimodal reasoning.
enables: This research opens directions for developing robust embodied AI systems that maintain safety guarantees under adversarial physical environments. It motivates the design of detection mechanisms and defenses against indirect prompt injection attacks in robotics and autonomous systems. The work highlights the need for adversarial training and verification methods specific to multimodal reasoning in embodied AI, while also informing policy and safety standards for deploying vision-language models in safety-critical applications like autonomous vehicles and aerial systems. Future work could explore certified defenses and provably robust architectures for embodied agents operating in adversarially manipulated environments.
